items.title: Christine Lagarde leaves door open to early ECB exit, as she mulls French politics
items.description: The European Central Bank's Christine Lagarde has declined to rule out an early end to her term as president, as she mulls a foray into French politics.

items.title: Gold prices set for first weekly rise in a month as investors scale back Fed rate hike bets
items.description: The metal was boosted as investors scaled back their bets on a looming rate hike.

items.title: Kuaishou shares jump after Tencent joins $2.8 billion raise for Kling AI subsidiary
items.description: Kuaishou shares rose as much as 6.89% at Friday's Hong Kong market open before paring gains to trade around 0.75% higher.

items.title: Extreme heat wave threatens U.S. power grids and July 4 travel
items.description: The heat wave threatens to overwhelm U.S. grids and may force some to change their plans on one of the busiest travel weeks of the year.

items.title: Oman walks a diplomatic tightrope over Strait of Hormuz fees, creating a ‘blind spot’ for markets
items.description: Oman is using strategic ambiguity as talks over possible Strait of Hormuz fees raise legal, diplomatic and oil market concerns.

items.title: Rafael Nadal talks tennis prize money, his hotels, and what sports taught him about business
items.description: Rafael Nadal opened his fourth Zel hotel as he expands into hospitality, education and sports after retiring from tennis.

items.title: Venezuela quake toll climbs to 2,595 as damage estimates mount after strongest tremor in a century
items.description: Death toll from twin earthquakes that struck Venezuela jumped to 2,595, as rescue operations continued after the country's strongest tremors in over a century.

items.title: Nvidia offers start-up customers chance to swap compute power for revenue share
items.description: Nvidia says it will give start-ups the chance to swap access to compute power for a slice of future profits under a new program aimed at AI-focused firms.

items.title: Jack Smith says he's 'very concerned what's going to happen next election' under Trump
items.description: Smith said that he could be indicted by the Department of Justice given Trump's animus toward him for prosecuting the president in two criminal cases.

items.title: Trump bought Apple, Nvidia and other tech giants before tariff reversal fueled rebound
items.description: The day before President Donald Trump walked back his tariff policy turned out to be one of his busiest days of 2025 for stock buying.

items.title: Saudi Arabia has ramped up oil shipments through the Strait of Hormuz since U.S.-Iran deal
items.description: Saudi Arabia is restarting its export logistics in the Persian Gulf after a three-month pause, according to Kpler.

items.title: Tesla stock sinks 7% despite strong deliveries report, posting worst day in nearly a year
items.description: Tesla is trying to recover from consecutive annual declines in vehicle sales that were partly caused by a consumer backlash against CEO Elon Musk.

items.title: Olympian David Hearn indicted in Reflecting Pool destruction of property case
items.description: President Donald Trump has made the renovation of the Reflecting Pool on the National Mall a top priority in his second year back in the White House.

items.title: Trump Accounts get a boost from employer contributions — Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley are the latest to offer matching programs
items.description: The growing list of companies that will match contributions to Trump Accounts for their employees now includes Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley.

items.title: Levi's, The North Face and Columbia are turning to women to fuel their next phase of growth
items.description: Legacy apparel brands including VF Corp., Levi's, and Columbia are investing more heavily in women's products and marketing.

items.title: Job seekers giving up: Labor force participation rate falls to lowest in 50 years, outside of Covid era
items.description: A drop in the unemployment rate helped provide some upside to what was an otherwise downbeat jobs report — but it was for all the wrong reasons.

items.title: GE Vernova's gas turbines aren't the only way it's winning from the AI boom
items.description: Electrification secured $2.4 billion in data center equipment orders during the first quarter of 2026, beating the segment's total for all of 2025.

items.title: Sandwich chain Jersey Mike's files for IPO, reports 50% same-store sales growth in recent years
items.description: Jersey Mike's has nearly 3,300 locations, making it the second-largest hoagie sandwich chain in the U.S. behind Subway.

items.title: Premier Lacrosse League plans to bring in team owners by 2028 'or soon thereafter,' co-founder says
items.description: Paul Rabil told CNBC he's banking on the 2028 Los Angeles Summer Games, where lacrosse will return as an Olympic sport, to shine a spotlight on the PLL.

items.title: House lawmakers approved a bipartisan bill to protect older adults from financial fraud. Here's what to know
items.description: The Financial Exploitation Prevention Act of 2025 is aimed at preventing adults age 65 or older or those with disabilities from losing their money to fraud.
